name: Integration and Governance Core Dictionary
plain_description: A structured dictionary for the platform's LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy governance model. It describes tool registrations, launches, LTI Advantage services, OAuth clients, scopes, privacy rules, consent, retention, and audit records exposed as SQL and JSON.
model_scope: Launch and service metadata, OAuth/OIDC security configuration, field-level scope policy, privacy rule accounting, consent records, retention rules, and audit trails. This is not a formal LTI, Security Framework, or Data Privacy certification claim.

oauth_scope_policies.plain_description: Read LTI tool registration records and their system configuration fields.
Read tenant deployment and service endpoint records for configured tools.
Read launch validation history, including hashed tokens and retained claim records for audit review.
Read LTI Names and Role Provisioning membership fields for the current context.
Read LTI Assignment and Grade Services line item fields without learner result values.
Read LTI Assignment and Grade Services learner result fields for an allowed line item.
Submit score updates for allowed LTI Assignment and Grade Services line items.
Create or read LTI Deep Linking item selections for a launch-bound instructor workflow.
Read OAuth client and scope policy records used to evaluate least-privilege access.
Read privacy sharing and retention policy records that decide whether fields may be exposed.
Read consent records and evidence pointers for privacy-governed sharing decisions.
Read privacy audit events, including actor, client, target, purpose, outcome, and timing fields.
Read only directory-level OneRoster user and organization fields.
Read class and enrollment fields that identify a person's learning context.
Read OneRoster gradebook line item and result fields for authorized classes.

objects.name: LTI Registration
objects.plain_description: The security agreement between this platform and one external LTI tool.
objects.why_it_exists: A registration stores the issuer, client ID, launch URLs, key material references, and service scopes a tool may use before any course deployment can launch it.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this LTI registration.
The school, district, or customer boundary that owns this registration.
The platform issuer identifier used in LTI launch tokens.
The OAuth client ID assigned to the tool by the platform.
The name administrators and instructors see for the external tool.
The tool URL where the platform starts the LTI login flow.
The tool URLs that are allowed to receive launch responses.
The URL where public signing keys for the tool or platform can be fetched.
The platform URL used during OpenID Connect authentication.
The platform URL where the tool asks for service access tokens.
The service permissions the tool may request after policy review.
Whether this registration can currently be used.
When this registration was created.
When this registration was last changed.

objects.name: LTI Deployment
objects.plain_description: One installation of an LTI tool for a tenant, school, course, section, or activity.
objects.why_it_exists: Deployments scope which registration can launch in which learning context and which services are available there.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this deployment.
The LTI registration that governs this deployment.
The immutable deployment identifier sent in LTI launch claims.
The level where this tool installation is available.
The course, section, school, or tenant context this deployment is attached to.
The specific course activity link this deployment is tied to when the tool is activity-scoped.
The LTI Advantage scopes enabled for this deployment after tenant policy filtering.
Whether launches and services for this deployment are enabled.
When this deployment was created.

objects.name: LTI Launch
objects.plain_description: One launch attempt from the platform to an external LTI tool.
objects.why_it_exists: Launch records preserve who launched, from which class or activity, which tool received the launch, and how the signed claims were validated.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this launch attempt.
The tenant boundary that owns the launch event.
The registration used to validate and route the launch.
The LTI deployment identifier included in the launch.
The LTI workflow represented by this launch.
The LTI version claim supplied in the launch.
The user identifier from the launch token when the launch is not anonymous.
The course, section, or group where the launch happened.
The LTI roles supplied for the launching user.
The final tool URL that should receive the launch.
A stored fingerprint of the one-time launch nonce used to prevent replay.
A fingerprint of the signed launch token kept for audit without exposing the full token everywhere.
The original launch claims retained for troubleshooting and conformance review under tenant policy.
When the launch was initiated.
Whether signature, issuer, audience, deployment, nonce, and time checks passed.

objects.name: LTI Service Endpoint
objects.plain_description: An LTI Advantage service URL that a tool may call for roster, gradebook, or deep-linking workflows.
objects.why_it_exists: Services such as NRPS, AGS, and Deep Linking need explicit URLs, versions, scopes, and policy gates before a tool can call them.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this LTI service endpoint.
The deployment that may expose this service endpoint.
Which LTI Advantage service this endpoint supports.
The API URL a tool calls for this service.
The supported service versions advertised to the tool.
The OAuth scope required before the tool may call this endpoint.
The class, course, section, or activity context the service is scoped to.
Whether tenant privacy and scope policy allows this service to be exposed.

objects.name: LTI Membership
objects.plain_description: One person returned in an LTI Names and Role Provisioning Service roster response.
objects.why_it_exists: NRPS memberships let a tool understand who belongs to a launch context without copying the whole roster contract into every launch.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this LTI membership projection.
The NRPS endpoint that returned or would return this membership.
The course, section, group, or other context represented by the membership.
The LTI user ID for the member.
The resolved platform person ID when policy permits resolution.
The member name shared with the tool.
The member email shared with the tool if policy allows it.
The LTI roles this member has in the context.
Whether the membership is currently active.
A legacy or SIS-linked person identifier shared through LTI when allowed.

objects.name: LTI Grade Exchange
objects.plain_description: A gradebook line item, score, or result exchanged through LTI Assignment and Grade Services.
objects.why_it_exists: AGS activity needs a governed projection so tools can create gradebook columns, send scores, and read results without bypassing privacy and gradebook policy.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this AGS exchange record.
The AGS endpoint used for this grade exchange.
The URL or identifier for the gradebook line item.
The human-readable gradebook column name.
The maximum possible score for the line item or score update.
The points or score earned by the learner.
The learner who receives the score or result.
Where the learner is in the activity lifecycle.
Where the score is in the grading lifecycle.
When the tool or platform created this grade exchange event.
Whether the AGS exchange was accepted, rejected, or held for review.

objects.name: LTI Deep Link Item
objects.plain_description: One content item selected by an instructor from an LTI tool and returned to the platform.
objects.why_it_exists: Deep Linking lets tools add links, files, HTML snippets, or tool activities into course content while preserving placement and gradebook metadata.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this deep-linked item.
The Deep Linking launch that produced this selected item.
The kind of content the tool returned.
The title shown for the selected content item.
The content or tool URL returned by the tool.
An HTML snippet returned by the tool when the item is embedded content.
How the platform should display the selected item.
Gradebook line item details returned with the selected item.
When the item becomes available to learners.
When learner submissions for the item are due or closed.

objects.name: Security OAuth Client
objects.plain_description: An OAuth client record used for LTI tools or other API integrations.
objects.why_it_exists: Security Framework behavior needs a governed client registry for token issuance, key validation, scopes, and lifecycle status.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this OAuth client.
The tenant that owns or authorizes this client.
The public OAuth client identifier.
The name shown to administrators when reviewing the client.
The URL where the client's public keys can be fetched.
A stored public key used to verify client assertions when a JWKS URL is not used.
How the client proves its identity to the token endpoint.
The OAuth grant types this client is allowed to use.
The scopes this client may request after tenant and privacy policy are applied.
Whether tokens may currently be issued for this client.

objects.name: Security Scope Policy
objects.plain_description: A rule mapping an OAuth scope to the API resources, actions, roles, and privacy classes it may access.
objects.why_it_exists: Scopes should mean more than endpoint names; they need a machine-readable connection to dictionary fields and privacy limits.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this scope policy.
The OAuth scope controlled by this rule.
The endpoint group or dictionary object this scope may access.
The operation permitted by this scope.
The most sensitive privacy class this scope may expose without another policy rule.
Optional dictionary field names or patterns allowed by this scope.
Whether access must be tied to a current launch, deployment, class, or activity context.
Roles that may use or benefit from the scope.
Whether this scope policy is active, inactive, or retired.

objects.name: Privacy Data Sharing Rule
objects.plain_description: A tenant policy rule explaining what data may be shared, with whom, for what purpose, and under what legal basis.
objects.why_it_exists: Data Privacy requirements need field-level sharing decisions that can be audited and connected to scopes, tools, and API responses.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this data sharing rule.
The tenant whose policy this rule belongs to.
The broad kind of school data governed by the rule.
The platform privacy class for the data covered by this rule.
Who may receive the data under this rule.
The educational or operational reason for sharing the data.
fields.school_example: Provide roster context for classroom practice activity.

fields.plain_description: The legal or policy basis used to justify the data sharing.
The dictionary fields or field patterns this rule covers.
How the platform limits shared data to what the purpose requires.
fields.school_example: Share names and roles only; withhold email unless separately approved.

fields.plain_description: Whether this sharing rule is currently active.
When the rule was last reviewed.

objects.name: Privacy Consent Record
objects.plain_description: A record of learner, guardian, or administrator consent connected to a privacy rule.
objects.why_it_exists: Some sharing workflows require explicit consent state that can be checked before exposing data to tools or exports.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this consent record.
The tenant boundary for this consent record.
The learner or user whose data the consent concerns.
The guardian or authorized person who gave consent when applicable.
The data sharing rule this consent applies to.
Whether consent is granted, denied, revoked, expired, or pending.
When the consent state became effective.
When the consent state expires if it is time-limited.
How the platform received this consent state.
A reference to the form, agreement, or audit evidence for the consent.

objects.name: Privacy Retention Rule
objects.plain_description: A tenant rule for how long a category of data is retained and what happens when it expires.
objects.why_it_exists: Retention rules make it clear how raw launches, tokens, events, scores, consent records, and audit data should be minimized or deleted.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this retention rule.
The tenant whose data retention policy this rule belongs to.
The standard or platform area that produces the data.
The category of data controlled by this retention rule.
How many days the data may be retained before the action is due.
What the platform should do when data reaches the retention limit.
How the platform should retain raw standards payloads after projection.
Whether deletion or anonymization is paused by a legal hold.
Whether this retention rule is active, inactive, or retired.

objects.name: Privacy Audit Event
objects.plain_description: An audit trail entry for access, sharing, export, deletion, policy changes, consent changes, or token issuance involving governed data.
objects.why_it_exists: Privacy and security controls must leave evidence of who accessed data, why it was allowed or denied, and what policy was applied.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this audit event.
The tenant boundary for the audited event.
The person who performed the audited action, if a person was involved.
The OAuth client or tool that performed the audited action, if an integration was involved.
The type of privacy or security event being recorded.
The dictionary object, endpoint group, or policy area affected by the event.
The specific record affected by the event when one applies.
The most sensitive privacy class involved in the event.
The scope, sharing, consent, or retention policy that governed the event.
When the audited event occurred.
Whether the action was allowed, denied, failed, or completed.
A short explanation for the audit outcome.
fields.school_example: Scope and data sharing rule allowed roster names for this deployment.

record_status.label: Active
record_status.plain_description: The record is currently allowed for use.

record_status.label: Inactive
record_status.plain_description: The record exists but is not currently usable.

record_status.label: Retired
record_status.plain_description: The record is kept for history but should not be used for new activity.

deployment_scope.label: Tenant
deployment_scope.plain_description: The deployment applies across the whole school, district, or customer boundary.

deployment_scope.label: School
deployment_scope.plain_description: The deployment applies to one school or building.

deployment_scope.label: Course
deployment_scope.plain_description: The deployment applies to one course offering.

deployment_scope.label: Section
deployment_scope.plain_description: The deployment applies to one class or section.

deployment_scope.label: Activity
deployment_scope.plain_description: The deployment applies to one resource link or learning activity.

lti_message_type.label: Resource link launch
lti_message_type.plain_description: A normal launch from a course activity link.

lti_message_type.label: Deep Linking request
lti_message_type.plain_description: An instructor launch used to select content from a tool.

lti_message_type.label: Deep Linking response
lti_message_type.plain_description: A tool response returning selected content to the platform.

lti_version.label: LTI 1.3
lti_version.plain_description: The LTI 1.3 launch message version used by the current model.

validation_status.label: Pending
validation_status.plain_description: The payload or workflow has not been fully checked yet.

validation_status.label: Valid
validation_status.plain_description: The payload or workflow passed platform validation.

validation_status.label: Invalid
validation_status.plain_description: The payload or workflow failed validation.

validation_status.label: Rejected
validation_status.plain_description: The platform rejected the payload or workflow under policy.

service_type.label: Names and Role Provisioning
service_type.plain_description: The service that returns course or context memberships.

service_type.label: AGS line items
service_type.plain_description: The service surface for gradebook columns.

service_type.label: AGS scores
service_type.plain_description: The service surface for sending score updates.

service_type.label: AGS results
service_type.plain_description: The service surface for reading stored grade results.

service_type.label: Deep Linking
service_type.plain_description: The launch workflow for selecting tool content into a course.

membership_status.label: Active
membership_status.plain_description: The person is currently active in the context.

membership_status.label: Inactive
membership_status.plain_description: The person has a membership but is not currently active.

activity_progress.label: Initialized
activity_progress.plain_description: The activity record exists but work has not started.

grading_progress.label: Pending
grading_progress.plain_description: The grade is not ready yet.

grading_progress.label: Pending manual
grading_progress.plain_description: A person still needs to grade the work.

grading_progress.label: Not ready
grading_progress.plain_description: There is not enough information to grade yet.

deep_link_item_type.label: File
deep_link_item_type.plain_description: A file selected from the tool.

deep_link_item_type.label: HTML
deep_link_item_type.plain_description: An HTML snippet selected from the tool.

deep_link_item_type.label: LTI resource link
deep_link_item_type.plain_description: A launchable LTI activity returned by the tool.

deep_link_item_type.label: Image
deep_link_item_type.plain_description: An image resource selected from the tool.

document_target.label: Iframe
document_target.plain_description: Display the item in an embedded frame.

document_target.label: Window
document_target.plain_description: Open the item in a new browser window or tab.

document_target.label: Embed
document_target.plain_description: Place the item directly in page content.

auth_method.label: Private key JWT
auth_method.plain_description: The client signs a JWT with its private key to authenticate.

auth_method.label: Client secret basic
auth_method.plain_description: The client authenticates with a shared secret using basic authentication.

auth_method.label: Client secret post
auth_method.plain_description: The client authenticates with a shared secret in the token request body.

client_status.label: Suspended
client_status.plain_description: Token issuance is paused for this client.

client_status.label: Retired
client_status.plain_description: The client is retained for history but should not receive new tokens.

scope_action.label: Read
scope_action.plain_description: The scope allows data to be read.

scope_action.label: Write
scope_action.plain_description: The scope allows data to be created or changed.

scope_action.label: Launch
scope_action.plain_description: The scope or policy supports launch-bound access.

privacy_class_value.label: Public
privacy_class_value.plain_description: Safe to publish without exposing a private learner record.

privacy_class_value.label: Directory
privacy_class_value.plain_description: Name, email, picture, role, or organization information that schools often share but still control.

privacy_class_value.label: Education record
privacy_class_value.plain_description: Enrollment, grade, progress, submission, achievement, or transcript data about a learner.

privacy_class_value.label: Sensitive
privacy_class_value.plain_description: Data needing extra care, including private identifiers, demographics, accommodations, or security proof material.

privacy_class_value.label: Behavioral
privacy_class_value.plain_description: Activity or usage data created by using a tool or resource.

privacy_class_value.label: Operational
privacy_class_value.plain_description: Import, export, launch, validation, or processing data.

privacy_class_value.label: System
privacy_class_value.plain_description: Platform configuration, keys, tokens, scopes, secrets metadata, or infrastructure data.

privacy_class_value.label: Privacy governance
privacy_class_value.plain_description: Policy and review data used to decide how other data may be shared or retained.

data_category.label: Roster
data_category.plain_description: People, roles, classes, sections, and memberships.

data_category.label: Gradebook
data_category.plain_description: Line items, scores, results, and grading state.

data_category.label: Assessment
data_category.plain_description: Assessment content, attempts, responses, and outcomes.

data_category.label: Activity
data_category.plain_description: Launches, events, sessions, and tool usage.

data_category.label: Credential
data_category.plain_description: Badges, transcripts, achievements, endorsements, and proofs.

data_category.label: Identifier
data_category.plain_description: IDs and crosswalks that connect people or resources across systems.

data_category.label: Security
data_category.plain_description: Tokens, keys, scopes, client records, and audit evidence.

data_category.label: Raw payload
data_category.plain_description: Original standards payloads retained for audit or reprocessing.

recipient_type.label: District admin
recipient_type.plain_description: A district administrator or authorized central-office user.

recipient_type.label: School staff
recipient_type.plain_description: A teacher, counselor, administrator, or staff member.

recipient_type.label: Learner
recipient_type.plain_description: The learner whose data is involved.

recipient_type.label: Guardian
recipient_type.plain_description: A parent or guardian authorized to view the learner's data.

recipient_type.label: Service provider
recipient_type.plain_description: A vendor or processor acting under agreement.

legal_basis.label: Consent
legal_basis.plain_description: The person or guardian has consented to this sharing.

legal_basis.label: Contract
legal_basis.plain_description: The sharing is covered by a contract or service agreement.

legal_basis.label: School official
legal_basis.plain_description: The recipient acts under school official or equivalent educational interest rules.

legal_basis.label: Legitimate interest
legal_basis.plain_description: The sharing supports an approved operational or educational interest.

legal_basis.label: Legal obligation
legal_basis.plain_description: The sharing is required by law, regulation, or formal process.

consent_status.label: Pending
consent_status.plain_description: The platform has not received a final consent choice.

consent_status.label: Expired
consent_status.plain_description: Consent was time-limited and is no longer active.

consent_source_method.label: Administrator
consent_source_method.plain_description: An administrator recorded the consent state.

consent_source_method.label: Guardian portal
consent_source_method.plain_description: A guardian submitted the consent state through a portal.

consent_source_method.label: Learner portal
consent_source_method.plain_description: A learner submitted the consent state through a portal.

consent_source_method.label: Imported agreement
consent_source_method.plain_description: The consent state came from an imported agreement or external system.

source_standard.label: LTI
source_standard.plain_description: Learning Tools Interoperability launch or service data.

source_standard.label: Security Framework
source_standard.plain_description: OAuth, OIDC, key, token, scope, or service security data.

source_standard.label: Data Privacy
source_standard.plain_description: Privacy policy, consent, retention, or audit data.

source_standard.label: OneRoster
source_standard.plain_description: Rostering, gradebook, or related OneRoster data.

retention_action.label: Delete
retention_action.plain_description: Remove the data when the retention limit is reached.

retention_action.label: Anonymize
retention_action.plain_description: Remove or transform identifiers so the data no longer identifies a learner.

retention_action.label: Aggregate only
retention_action.plain_description: Keep only aggregate counts or summaries.

retention_action.label: Legal hold
retention_action.plain_description: Retain the data because a legal hold prevents deletion.

raw_payload_policy.label: Retain
raw_payload_policy.plain_description: Keep the original payload under access controls.

raw_payload_policy.label: Minimize
raw_payload_policy.plain_description: Retain only the payload parts needed for audit or reprocessing.

raw_payload_policy.label: Hash only
raw_payload_policy.plain_description: Keep a fingerprint of the payload instead of the full original.

raw_payload_policy.label: Discard after projection
raw_payload_policy.plain_description: Delete the raw payload after structured fields are projected.

privacy_event_type.label: Export
privacy_event_type.plain_description: Data was exported outside the platform.

privacy_event_type.label: Share
privacy_event_type.plain_description: Data was shared with a recipient or tool.

privacy_event_type.label: Delete
privacy_event_type.plain_description: Data was deleted or scheduled for deletion.

privacy_event_type.label: Policy change
privacy_event_type.plain_description: A privacy, scope, or retention policy changed.

privacy_event_type.label: Token issue
privacy_event_type.plain_description: A security token was issued or denied.

unsupported_or_deferred.reason: A minimal authenticated launch receipt path exists. Production launch validation requires key management, nonce/state storage, deployment records, signature validation, and certification fixtures.

unsupported_or_deferred.area: Public AGS, NRPS, and Deep Linking runtime services
unsupported_or_deferred.reason: Need production token issuance, tenant row-level authorization, gradebook write policy, and service-specific conformance tests before public exposure.
A minimal authenticated token-exchange receipt path exists. Production token issuance requires server-side auth state, client assertion validation, and managed secret/key storage.

The dictionary and audit slices model policy and sensitive-read audit rows. Automated consent checks, retention jobs, deletion, export, and data-sharing agreements need broader backend workflow services.
LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy certification require conformance testing and organizational review beyond generated dictionary artifacts.
